reviews.review_description: I deducted one star because of missing features. Please consider adding the following: 1- While playing, the actual picture is nowhere to be found. We have to solve by memory or click away from the puzzle to see the whole picture. 2- Add a timer, so we know how long it took to solve any puzzle. 3- Add a button so we can choose to have mixed rotation of pieces to make it a bit more challenging at any level. At the moment, all puzzle pieces start at upright position. Cheers!

I've got to say, this is really cool. It's just like real puzzles, but in virtual reality (mixed reality for this version). Very intuitive to play (once you get used to the menus, although that was easy for me). I love how they look like puzzle pieces even after the pieces are placed together. Lots of vibrant puzzles to choose from.

For some, the 25-100 piece counts may not be challenging enough. But there are a lot of puzzles included, and it can be refreshing and low-pressure to do puzzles of this size.

The two suggestions I would have would be [1] to add more "stock photo" or similar puzzles (most of the puzzles look to me like they were AI generated, although done well and detailed), and [2] to have the pieces at the beginning all separate from each other (for 100 piece puzzles, there are often quite a few pieces that start on top of each other, but it is easy to move them elsewhere).

reviews.review_title: I like both MR and VR versions
reviews.review_description: I caught them both in sale last month.  It is same game, but I like the atmosphere of VR and when time is short, I prefer the MR version.   I did not realize I would play these games as much as I am.

Love all kinds of puzzles. Have played almost all of the puzzle games available on PC as well as the quest headset. This is a traditional,down to earth: jigsaw puzzle game ('nuf said). However, for the life of me I cannot figure out why the developer would put out two of the same games and the only difference between the two is the augmented reality? 🤔. I mean if a game like puzzling places could implement an augmented reality button then why couldn't it be the same for this game? It would have made sense if there was a difference between the two games but there is none apart from the aforementioned AR mode. Had to refund the basic game and stuck with the augmented reality version.  Still it is a good basic jigsaw puzzle game with various difficulty levels.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed feedback! We understand your point about having two versions of the game and appreciate your suggestions. Splitting the versions was a deliberate choice to offer specific options, but we'll consider the possibility of integrating them in the future. We're glad you're enjoying the AR mode and the various difficulty levels. Thanks for playing! 😊

reviews.review_description:
It’s the same puzzle as the other games. No difference. I play both and no change. Would be nice if I can pick what puzzles to play. It always puts the same one up that I’ve already played. Is there a way to pick one that I have not played yes. Yes I tried the hard ones etc. maybe I’m doing something wrong. Thank you . I love puzzles. I actually do the other one. 
Keep up the good work

developer_response.body: There are over 40 puzzles available! Just hold down the right trigger and scroll through the menu to see them all. Additionally, in the store tab, you can unlock even more puzzles by playing and earning in-game coins. Happy puzzling! 🧩✨

reviews.review_description:
I have enjoyed playing the game.    Hard mode is indeed hard (I wasn't sure how I would be challenged in a 25 piece game, but yes, this is indeed possible).  

My issue is that there are too many puzzles that I'm not even interested in doing because the picture is so unappealing.     It feels like the playtime is limited because there are so few appealing puzzles.   How about allowing me to add my own photos?   Or using photos of travel locations (like many puzzles and electronic puzzles do).

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! Glad you’re enjoying Hard mode—25 pieces can be tricky! We dare you to tackle a 200-piece puzzle in Hard mode 😜

As for the images, we hear you. Adding travel and real-world locations is a great idea, and we’ll include them in the next update. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
Great puzzler.
Menu needs a bitmore intuivity, took me quite some while to figure out I could swipe the puzzles with trigger down. >.<
Would be nice if the puzzle could be locked to table trough AR.
Also import own pictures to be slices up?

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! We're glad you enjoy the puzzles! 😊 We'll look into making the menu more intuitive to improve usability. Locking the puzzle to the table in AR and adding a feature to import custom pictures are great suggestions—definitely something to consider for future updates. Thanks for sharing your thoughts! 🎮✨

reviews.review_description:
I've played this a little bit and it's nice and relaxing. My main frustration is that you cannot see the top of the puzzle box unless you pause the game. I really think there should be an option to see the picture you're making without having to pause. In real life you can hold a jigsaw puzzle piece and look at the box to figure out where the piece would go. 
It would be great if there was multiplayer, ideally with co-location, so I can play this with me wife. You get coins when you complete a puzzle, but I can only see 4 puzzles available to unlock, which I don't think is the full collection. I'm not sure why I can't view them all?

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! We’ll consider adding an option to view the puzzle box during gameplay.

For multiplayer, the online version of the game, called Oasis Puzzles, might be just what you’re looking for. Also, you can scroll the puzzle menu by dragging—there are about 70 puzzles to explore. Hope that helps! 😊

reviews.review_description: Beautiful puzzles. And good amount of content.

developer_response.body: Thank you Abhim. More puzzles and features coming soon!

reviews.review_description: I really enjoy these puzzles as I can play in augmented mode while having the TV on in the background. The puzzles are beautiful and so far seem to have new ones every time I play. Please add an option where you can view the box image of the puzzle while assembling, it would be very helpful, especially with the harder pieces.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! Some players enjoy not seeing the box while playing. However, you can already see the box by pressing the pause button. We will consider adding an option to view the box during gameplay.

reviews.review_description:
Love the variety of puzzles. Enjoyable and relaxing.

Please make it so that I can move the puzzles and pieces up and down. Hard to do puzzles unless at correct height. I like to be able to do laying or sitting. Also would like pieces more spread out.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We’ll be adding the ability to move the puzzle up and down for better height adjustment. We'll also consider your suggestion about spreading the pieces out more. We appreciate your input!

reviews.review_title: Nice game but maybe just an AR copy?
reviews.review_description: Oasis puzzle is a nice addition to the amazing Puzzling Places. If you are more into jigsaw puzzles with nice motives, then you should give it a try. But it looks to me like this game differs from the other Oasis puzzle app only in so far that it is played in Passthrough mode. So far i do not really understand why the developers have not just integrated AR mode into the other (main) app. If there are any other differences that justify to buy another game, then 5 stars will be justified.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, Schluehunter. Yes, Oasis Puzzles AR is an AR version of Oasis Puzzles. We have different plans for both games, so in the future, they will have more distinct features. We will update the game description to make this clearer.

reviews.review_description: I expected like 5-8 puzzles not 50. And I can unlock them all by using ingame currency I get after just playing the game! The only thing is a little strange for me is that the pieces are not rotated on medium difficulty. And a local mixed reality multiplayer mode like in Puzzling Places would be awesome. But still 10/10 game for the price.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! We’re glad you’re enjoying the game. Medium difficulty doesn’t have rotation as it’s the default mode, and we wanted to avoid wrist strain. We’ll keep local multiplayer in mind for the future. Happy puzzling! 😊

reviews.review_description: Words can not explain how much I hate the pass-through.  I had hoped there was an option to turn it off. Makes it practically unplayable to me.

developer_response.body: Hi! We're really sorry to hear that the passthrough mode is causing issues for you. Since this game is focused on the Augmented Reality experience, we wanted to let you know that we also have a standard version of the game called Oasis Puzzles, which features fully 3D environments without passthrough and includes an online mode. This version might be a better fit for you. Thanks for your feedback!

reviews.review_description: Let you inner child out and simply play. only thing is board positioning. know your working on it- but so important.

developer_response.body: Hello, thanks for your feedback! You can move the board relative to yourself by holding it and repositioning it!
